events.label: reasoning
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 50,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Epoxy grout applied where cementitious grout was specified (or vice versa), violating engineered specification.",
    "Grout misapplication can compromise baseplate support, alignment, and load transfer.",
    "Work is in-progress with defective installation, posing immediate risk to structural integrity."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately on the affected baseplates.",
    "Remove the misapplied grout per manufacturer and specification requirements.",
    "Verify substrate preparation and re-apply correct grout type as per API RP 686 and project specs.",
    "Inspect anchor bolt alignment and tension before re-grouting.",
    "Document non-conformance and obtain engineering approval before proceeding."
  ],
  "rationale": "API RP 686 requires strict adherence to grout type and installation procedures for machinery baseplates. Misapplication of epoxy vs. cementitious grout is a critical defect that can lead to equipment misalignment, vibration, and failure. Work cannot continue until the defective grout is removed and replaced correctly.",
  "references": ["API RP 686", "Project specifications for grout type"]
}
